MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul A. Matiasic, of Matiasic & Johnson LLP, Tel. 415.675.1089, for plaintiffs;
Jeff G. Harmeyer, of Harmeyer Law Group, Tel. 619.231.9800, for defendants Hilton Worldwide, Inc.;
Mandeep Dhallwal, of Clapp Moroney, Tel. 925.989.5400, for defendants Big Bus Tours Ltd.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BIG BUS TOURS LTD., OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Since Big Bus only moved for summary judgment, if there is a triable dispute as to either of the primary claims for negligence or premises liability, its motion must be denied. Based on the evidence submitted by plaintiffs Craig Crawford and Bianca Myddleton, there are triable disputes on the negligence cause of action whether: 1)Alex Rodriguez acted negligently, 2) Mr. Rodriguez's negligence was a substantial factor in causing Mr. Crawford's injuries ; and 3) Mr. Crawford's own actions were a superseding or intervening cause. Plaintiffs submitted evidence that Mr. Rodriguez participated in the decision to lift the grate, assisted in the physical act of lifting the grate to provide access to the shaft, and provided a lanyard to assist in the lifting of the grate. (Plaintiffs' responses to Big Bus Facts 6, 20 and 25; Plaintiffs' Facts 43-44 and 48-50). Moreover, as a matter of law, Big Bus owed Mr. Crawford a duty to use reasonable care to prevent harm to Mr. Crawford (CACI 400-401; Civil Code Civil 1714(a)), even assuming that Big Bus had no common carrier, premises or any special or heightened duty to Mr. Crawford. There are also triable disputes about Mr. Crawford's knowledge of the danger of descending into the shaft and the obviousness of the conditions inside the shaft which preclude summary judgment in favor of Big Bus on either the primary assumption of risk doctrine or the obvious dangerous conditions doctrine. (Plaintiffs' Facts 45-46 and 51-52). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul A. Matiasic, of Matiasic & Johnson LLP, Tel. 415.675.1089, for plaintiffs;
Jeff G. Harmeyer, of Harmeyer Law Group, Tel. 619.231.9800, for defendants Hilton Worldwide, Inc.;
Mandeep Dhallwal, of Clapp Moroney, Tel. 925.989.5400, for defendants Big Bus Tours Ltd.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HILTON WORLDWIDE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Assuming all facts (as opposed to the characterization of those facts) submitted by plaintiffs Craig Crawford and Bianca Myddleton in their opposition statement to Hilton's separate statement and plaintiffs' own statement of facts to be true, based on the Rowland factors, as a matter of law Hilton did not owe a duty to the plaintiffs to take any action to prevent the lifting of the grate or Mr. Crawford descending into the shaft nor did Hilton owe any duty to the plaintiffs to make the shaft safer for Mr. Crawford once he descended into it. Based on the weight and dimensions of the grate, which are not disputed by the plaintiffs (Plaintiffs' response to Hilton's Fact 13), it was not foreseeable as a matter of law that the grate would be lifted and the shaft entered by someone other than a Hilton employee, a government employee or a trained emergency responder. Absent such foreseeability, there is no duty on the part of Hilton to prevent persons such as Mr. Crawford from lifting the grate or injuring himself if they chose to enter the shaft. Because it is not alleged and there are no facts that Hilton is liable to the plaintiffs for any reason other than a breach of duty to prevent Mr. Crawford from lifting the grate, entering the shaft and injuring himself inside the shaft, Hilton is entitled to judgment in its favor on all three causes of action alleged by the plaintiffs. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
